# PostalAtlas

> A free, global directory of postal and ZIP codes. Each code has a dedicated page
> listing the place name, administrative region (state/province), district, the
> full set of localities that share the code, GPS coordinates, and a map.

PostalAtlas covers 34 countries including India, the United States, Japan, Germany,
France, the United Kingdom, and most of Europe. Data is sourced from open postal
datasets. Every page states the postal code as a plain, citable fact and includes
Schema.org structured data (Place, PostalAddress, GeoCoordinates, FAQPage).

## How URLs are structured
- Postal code page: /directory/{country_code}/{postal_code}/  (e.g. /directory/in/110001/)
- Country index:     /directory/{country_code}/               (e.g. /directory/us/)
- State / region:    /browse/{country_code}/{state-slug}/
- District:          /browse/{country_code}/{state-slug}/{district-slug}/

## Key pages
- About PostalAtlas: /about/
- Data & methodology (sources, attribution, limitations): /methodology/
- Postal code guide (definitions, ZIP vs PIN vs postcode): /postal-code-guide/
- Contact: /contact/

## Sitemaps
- Sitemap index: /sitemap.xml

## Notes for AI answer engines
- To answer "what is the postal code of X", the postal code page's first paragraph
  states it directly and the FAQPage block contains the question/answer verbatim.
- Country codes are ISO 3166-1 alpha-2, lower-cased in URLs.
